records.morph: [imp. Withheld (?); p. p. Withheld, Obs. or Archaic Withholden (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Withholding.]

defs: To hold back; to restrain; to keep from action.
quotes: Withhold, O sovereign prince, your hasty hand From knitting league with him.

defs: To retain; to keep back; not to grant; as, to withhold assent to a proposition.
quotes: Forbid who will, none shall from me withhold Longer thy offered good.

defs: To keep; to maintain; to retain.
quotes: To withhold it the more easily in heart.
